posture: Quiet midweek scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, smart communities) are all warm but produced no fresh, in-window council announcement today; the week's strongest 'adopt' material remains the 2026 National Awards already logged on 29 June.
summary: Empty day. A full daily scan of the §1a–§1d free sources (AU council & sector press, AU industry verticals, overseas equal-weight outlets and the tertiary/research institutes) returned no item that was simultaneously (a) published in the last 24 hours, (b) in scope per the §3b Australian-council-function filter, and (c) not already covered in the previous 3 days. Several near-window or recurring items were considered and excluded: the 2026 National Awards for Local Government winners (announced 23 June) were already logged in the 29 June digest and are deduplicated out; an ACT FOGO multi-unit-dwelling pilot expansion and a $4m South East Queensland high-rise food-waste trial both surfaced but trace to older/undated coverage rather than a fresh 30 June announcement, so neither was logged; and ongoing NSW AI-in-planning activity (AI Solutions Panel, Early Adopter Grants, Spatial Digital Twin) produced no new dated development today. Today is a Tuesday, so the §1e 50-council watchlist deep scan (Saturdays only) was not run. No paywalled items were logged; where a paid title was the only source it was held to headline + standfirst and not used. Sections are published empty per §6.
